name: Gateway of India
description: A 26-metre triumphal arch monument built in 1924 to commemorate the visit of King George V and Queen Mary. Overlooks the Arabian Sea and is the starting point for ferries to Elephanta Caves.
entry_fee_note: Free to view; ferry tickets to Elephanta cost extra

name: Elephanta Caves
description: UNESCO World Heritage rock-cut cave temples dating from the 5th-8th century CE, dedicated to Lord Shiva. Famous for the Trimurti sculpture. Reached by a 1-hour ferry from Gateway of India.
entry_fee_note: ₹40 for Indian nationals, ₹600 for foreign nationals; village entry ₹10 extra; ferry ₹200-250 round trip (not included)

name: Marine Drive
description: A 3.6km C-shaped boulevard along the coast, also called the Queen's Necklace for how its lights look at night from above. A favourite evening promenade for locals and tourists.

name: Girgaon Chowpatty
description: A busy sandy beach at the north end of Marine Drive, famous for street food stalls, sunset views, and as the site of Ganesh Chaturthi immersions.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya (CSMVS)
description: Mumbai's premier museum (formerly Prince of Wales Museum) housing over 50,000 artefacts spanning the Indus Valley, Gupta, and Mughal periods, plus natural history and decorative arts collections.
entry_fee_note: ₹85 for Indian adults; camera fee extra; children may have reduced rates

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CSMT)
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Victorian Gothic Revival railway station built in 1887, formerly Victoria Terminus. Still a fully functioning station and one of Mumbai's most photographed buildings.
entry_fee_note: Free to view exterior; functioning station
best_time: Evening (illuminated) or early morning for fewer crowds

name: Bandra-Worli Sea Link
description: A 5.6km cable-stayed bridge connecting Bandra and Worli across Mahim Bay, officially the Rajiv Gandhi Sea Link. Iconic at night when lit up; best experienced by car or cab.
entry_fee_note: Toll applies for vehicles (~₹85 one way for cars); free if viewed from Worli Sea Face
best_time: Night drive or sunset from Worli Sea Face

name: Haji Ali Dargah
description: A 19th-century mosque and tomb built on a tiny islet, accessible via a narrow causeway that is only walkable at low tide. One of Mumbai's most visited religious sites.
best_time: Morning or evening, check tide timings before visiting

name: Siddhivinayak Temple
description: One of Mumbai's most revered Ganesh temples, built in 1801. Hugely popular with devotees and celebrities; expect queues, especially on Tuesdays.
entry_fee_note: Free; VIP darshan tickets available for a fee on some days
best_time: Early morning to avoid queues (avoid Tuesdays if rushed)

name: Sanjay Gandhi National Park
description: A sprawling 100+ sq km protected forest within city limits, home to leopards, deer, 250+ bird species, boating lakes, a lion & tiger safari, and the ancient Kanheri Caves.
entry_fee_note: ~₹53 park entry for adults (varies); Lion/Tiger Safari ₹55 adults/₹22 children extra; boating ₹150-250; closed Mondays

name: Kanheri Caves
description: A complex of over 100 rock-cut Buddhist caves carved between the 1st century BCE and 10th century CE, used as monasteries and prayer halls. Located inside Sanjay Gandhi National Park.
entry_fee_note: ₹20 Indians (separate from SGNP park entry); shuttle bus from park entrance ~₹44 one-way

name: Juhu Beach
description: Mumbai's most famous beach, lined with food stalls selling pav bhaji, bhel puri and pani puri. Popular for evening walks, horse rides, and people-watching near celebrity homes.

name: ISKCON Temple (Sri Sri Radha Rasabihari Mandir)
description: A large, peaceful Krishna temple complex with marble architecture, regular aarti ceremonies, a vegetarian restaurant (Govinda's), and bookstore.

name: Colaba Causeway Market
description: A bustling street market stretching from Regal Cinema to Sassoon Dock, packed with stalls selling clothes, accessories, antiques, footwear and souvenirs at negotiable prices.
entry_fee_note: Free entry; bring cash for bargaining

name: Linking Road, Bandra
description: A long shopping street famous for trendy, budget clothing, footwear and accessories. Street stalls and branded outlets sit side by side.

name: Crawford Market (Mahatma Jyotiba Phule Mandai)
description: A 19th-century covered market hall designed in Norman-Gothic style, famous for fresh produce, spices, dry fruits, pets, and household goods.

name: Dharavi
description: One of Asia's largest urban settlements with a thriving informal economy of recycling, leather, pottery and textile industries. Guided walking tours offer respectful, educational insight.
entry_fee_note: Free to walk; guided tours typically ₹700-1500 per person, photography restricted in residential areas

name: Mahalaxmi Dhobi Ghat
description: A vast open-air laundromat where hundreds of dhobis (washermen) wash clothes in rows of concrete wash pens — a striking sight best viewed from the Mahalaxmi station bridge.
entry_fee_note: Free to view from the bridge; small fee may apply to walk inside with a guide
best_time: Morning, when washing is in full swing

name: Bademiya
description: A legendary late-night street food institution since 1946, famous for seekh kebabs, chicken tikka rolls and butter naan. Steps away from Taj Mahal Palace Hotel.
entry_fee_note: No entry fee; meal cost approx ₹300-500 per person

name: Khau Gully, CST
description: A famous food lane near CST station crammed with stalls serving vada pav, pav bhaji, sandwiches, and chaat — a true taste of Mumbai's street food culture.

name: Mohammed Ali Road Food Street
description: Mumbai's most famous Ramadan food street, transforming into a massive open-air feast of kebabs, biryani, malpua and sweets every evening during Ramadan, with many stalls active year-round.
entry_fee_note: No entry fee; meal cost ₹150-400 per person

name: Leopold Cafe
description: An iconic 1871-established cafe and bar in Colaba, popular with travellers and immortalised in the novel Shantaram. Known for its old-world charm and varied menu.
entry_fee_note: No entry fee; meal cost ₹600-1200 per person

name: Global Vipassana Pagoda
description: A massive golden dome inspired by Myanmar's Shwedagon Pagoda, built as a monument of peace and harmony. Houses the world's largest hollow stone dome without pillar support, with relics of the Buddha.
entry_fee_note: Free entry; reached via ferry from Gorai jetty (~₹10-30)

name: Adlabs Imagica Theme Park
description: India's first international-standard theme park with major rides like Wagon-O-O-Wheel, Nitro, and Rajasaurus River Adventure, plus a water park (Aquamagica) and snow park (Snomagica).
entry_fee_note: Single park tickets approx ₹1500-2000, combo packages higher; book online for discounts

name: Nehru Planetarium
description: A science museum and planetarium offering astronomy shows and exhibits on space science, popular with families and school groups.
entry_fee_note: Approx ₹100 for sky show; varies by show and age group

name: Nehru Science Centre
description: India's largest interactive science museum with hands-on exhibits covering physics, technology, and space exploration — great for curious minds of all ages.
entry_fee_note: Approx ₹90 for adults; combo with planetarium available

name: Hanging Gardens (Pherozeshah Mehta Gardens)
description: Terraced gardens atop Malabar Hill known for hedges shaped like animals, walking paths, and panoramic sunset views over the Arabian Sea and Marine Drive.

name: Kamala Nehru Park
description: A small hilltop garden famous for the 'Old Woman's Shoe' structure and one of the best viewpoints over Marine Drive and the Queen's Necklace at night.
best_time: Evening for the Marine Drive view

name: Worli Sea Face
description: A quieter seaside promenade offering views of the Bandra-Worli Sea Link, especially dramatic during monsoon when waves crash over the wall.

name: Bandra Fort (Castella de Aguada)
description: A 17th-century Portuguese watchtower ruin on a rocky outcrop overlooking the sea and the Bandra-Worli Sea Link — a compact but scenic heritage spot.

name: Mount Mary Church (Basilica of Our Lady of the Mount)
description: A 16th-century Roman Catholic basilica perched on a hill overlooking the sea, famous for its annual Bandra Fair (Mount Mary Fair) every September.
best_time: Morning or during the September fair

name: Bandstand Promenade
description: A seafront promenade near Bandra Fort, popular for evening walks and known for being close to several Bollywood celebrity residences, including Shah Rukh Khan's Mannat.

name: JVPD Scheme & Juhu Tara Road
description: An upscale residential area near Juhu, lined with celebrity bungalows, trendy cafes and boutiques — a popular spot for a relaxed walk.

name: Powai Lake
description: An artificial lake created in 1891, surrounded by upscale neighbourhoods, cafes, and walking paths — a calm escape with occasional bird sightings.

name: Dadasaheb Phalke Chitranagari (Film City)
description: Mumbai's iconic Bollywood film studio complex, with guided tours showing outdoor sets, gardens, and lakes used in countless films and shows.
entry_fee_note: Guided tour packages typically ₹600-1500 depending on duration and inclusions; advance booking recommended

name: Rajabai Clock Tower
description: A 280-foot Venetian Gothic clock tower within the University of Mumbai campus, part of the Victorian Gothic and Art Deco Ensembles UNESCO listing.
entry_fee_note: Free to view from outside; campus access may require permission

name: Bombay High Court
description: An 1870s Gothic Revival courthouse with two octagonal towers, one of South Mumbai's finest examples of colonial architecture. Visitors can view court proceedings.
entry_fee_note: Free; ID may be required for entry, security checks apply

name: Flora Fountain (Hutatma Chowk)
description: An ornately sculpted 1869 fountain at a busy traffic intersection in the financial district, surrounded by colonial-era buildings.

name: Kala Ghoda Art Precinct
description: An art and culture hub featuring galleries, the David Sassoon Library, street art, boutique cafes, and the annual Kala Ghoda Arts Festival every February.
entry_fee_note: Free to walk around; individual galleries/cafes charge separately
best_time: Daytime, especially during the February arts festival

name: Jehangir Art Gallery
description: One of India's most prominent art galleries, hosting rotating exhibitions of contemporary and modern Indian art since 1952. Adjacent to the Samovar Cafe.

name: Taj Mahal Palace Hotel
description: An opulent heritage hotel opened in 1903, an architectural landmark combining Moorish, Oriental and Florentine styles, located right next to the Gateway of India.
entry_fee_note: Free to view exterior; high-tea and dining experiences are paid

name: Fort Heritage Walk
description: A self-guided or organised walking route through Mumbai's Fort area covering Horniman Circle, St. Thomas Cathedral, Asiatic Society Library, and colonial-era arcades.
entry_fee_note: Free for self-guided; guided heritage walks typically ₹300-800

name: Horniman Circle Gardens
description: A landscaped circular garden in the heart of the Fort district, surrounded by Neo-Classical buildings including the Town Hall and Asiatic Society.

name: Veermata Jijabai Bhonsle Udyan (Byculla Zoo)
description: Mumbai's main zoo and botanical garden, home to the Bhau Daji Lad Museum and a popular family attraction with a variety of animals including a humboldt penguin enclosure.
entry_fee_note: Approx ₹25-50 for adults; closed on Wednesdays

name: Dr. Bhau Daji Lad Museum
description: Mumbai's oldest museum (established 1872), beautifully restored, showcasing the city's social and cultural history through art, photographs, and decorative objects.

name: Sassoon Dock
description: Mumbai's oldest dock, a working fishing harbour where colourful fishing boats unload their catch each morning — a raw, authentic slice of the city's maritime life.
entry_fee_note: Free; can be crowded and have strong fish odour

name: Antilia & Malabar Hill Walk
description: A scenic residential walk past Mumbai's most expensive properties, including Antilia (Mukesh Ambani's residence), with sea views along the way.
entry_fee_note: Free; public roads only, no entry to private property

name: Babulnath Temple
description: An ancient Shiva temple atop a small hill near Chowpatty, accessible by a flight of steps, with panoramic views of the city and sea.

name: Mani Bhavan Gandhi Museum
description: The house where Mahatma Gandhi stayed during his visits to Mumbai between 1917-1934, now a museum with photographs, letters, and a library dedicated to his life.

name: Marine Drive Art Deco Buildings
description: A row of pastel-coloured Art Deco apartment buildings from the 1930s-40s lining Marine Drive, part of Mumbai's UNESCO-listed Art Deco ensemble.
entry_fee_note: Free to view from the street
best_time: Daytime for photography, evening for ambience

name: Trishna
description: A celebrated seafood restaurant near Kala Ghoda, famous for its butter garlic crab and koliwada prawns — a favourite among food critics and tourists alike.
entry_fee_note: No entry fee; meal cost ₹1500-3000 per person

name: Elco Market Pani Puri Corner
description: A famous chaat hub inside Elco Arcade in Bandra, best known for its pani puri, ragda pattice, and other Mumbai chaat classics.

name: Shree Thaker Bhojanalay
description: An institution since 1945 serving authentic, unlimited Gujarati and Kathiawadi thalis in a traditional dining hall setting.
entry_fee_note: No entry fee; thali cost approx ₹500-700 per person

name: Phoenix Marketcity, Kurla
description: One of Mumbai's largest shopping malls with international and domestic brands, a multiplex, food court, and entertainment zones.
best_time: Afternoon to evening, avoid peak heat

name: High Street Phoenix & Palladium
description: A premium mall complex combining luxury retail (Palladium), a large food court, multiplex, and entertainment zones in a renovated mill compound.

name: Chor Bazaar
description: A famous flea market dating back over 150 years, known for antiques, vintage furniture, old Bollywood memorabilia, and curious second-hand finds.

name: Worli Koliwada (Fishing Village)
description: A traditional fishing community offering a glimpse into Mumbai's pre-colonial Koli heritage, with colourful boats, nets, and views of the Sea Link from the shore.

name: Versova Beach
description: A quieter beach known for its fishing community, sunset views, and recent coastal clean-up efforts that restored it as a nesting site for Olive Ridley turtles.

name: Madh Island
description: A laid-back coastal area with palm groves, a 16th-century Portuguese fort (Madh Fort), and quiet beaches popular for film shoots and weekend getaways.
entry_fee_note: Free entry; ferry from Versova adds to the experience

name: Vasai Fort (Bassein Fort)
description: A sprawling ruined Portuguese fort dating to 1536, overtaken by greenery, with old churches and bastions — a favourite for history buffs and photographers.

name: Gorai Beach
description: A clean, relatively uncrowded beach near the Global Vipassana Pagoda, popular for camel rides and a relaxed family day out.

name: Queen's Necklace Night View
description: The famous nighttime view of Marine Drive's curved streetlights resembling a string of pearls, best seen from Kamala Nehru Park or Malabar Hill.
best_time: After dark (lights switch on around sunset)

name: St. John the Evangelist Church (Afghan Church)
description: A striking Gothic-style church built in 1858 to commemorate soldiers who died in the First Afghan War, featuring stained glass windows and a peaceful courtyard.

name: Snow World
description: An indoor artificial snow park where visitors can experience snowfall, sledding, and snow play — a fun novelty especially for kids unfamiliar with snow.
entry_fee_note: Approx ₹600-900 per person depending on location and time slot

name: Mumbai Port Trust Garden (Sagar Upvan)
description: A large, well-maintained garden with a Japanese-style design, walking trails, a small lake, and views of the harbour — an underrated green escape.
